local_dishes.name: Jackfruit curry (polos)
local_dishes.description: Traditional Sri Lankan curry made with jackfruit
source_files: Srilanka/Mr. Shishir_ Sri Lanka_ All Inclusive Guide.pdf

local_dishes.name: Dhal Curry (Parippu)
local_dishes.description: Red lentils cooked with coconut milk, turmeric, onions, and mild spices, paired with Pol roti, string hoppers, or plain rice
source_files: Srilanka/Mr. Shishir_ Sri Lanka_ All Inclusive Guide.pdf

local_dishes.name: Pol Sambol
local_dishes.description: A spicy coconut relish made with freshly grated coconut, red chili, lime juice, and onions. Paired with hoppers or rice
source_files: Srilanka/Mr. Shishir_ Sri Lanka_ All Inclusive Guide.pdf

local_dishes.name: Hoppers (Appa)
local_dishes.description: Crispy, bowl-shaped pancakes made from fermented rice flour and coconut milk
source_files: Srilanka/Mr. Shishir_ Sri Lanka_ All Inclusive Guide.pdf

local_dishes.name: String Hoppers (Idiyappam)
local_dishes.description: Delicate rice noodle nests, typically served with dhal, coconut sambol, and vegetable curries
source_files: Srilanka/Mr. Shishir_ Sri Lanka_ All Inclusive Guide.pdf

local_dishes.name: Kiribath (Milk Rice)
local_dishes.description: Coconut milk rice cut into diamond shapes, often eaten for breakfast or special occasions. Paired with Lunu miris (chili-onion paste) or jaggery for a sweet version
source_files: Srilanka/Mr. Shishir_ Sri Lanka_ All Inclusive Guide.pdf

local_dishes.name: Vegetable Roti
local_dishes.description: Flatbread pockets filled with spiced vegetables, onions, and potatoes, then grilled or fried
source_files: Srilanka/Mr. Shishir_ Sri Lanka_ All Inclusive Guide.pdf

local_dishes.name: Eggplant Moju (Brinjal Moju)
local_dishes.description: Sweet, tangy, and spicy pickle-style eggplant dish, often served with rice and curry
source_files: Srilanka/Mr. Shishir_ Sri Lanka_ All Inclusive Guide.pdf

local_dishes.name: Gotukola Sambol
local_dishes.description: A refreshing herbal salad made from finely chopped gotukola (pennywort leaves), coconut, and lime
source_files: Srilanka/Mr. Shishir_ Sri Lanka_ All Inclusive Guide.pdf

local_dishes.name: Coconut Roti (Pol Roti)
local_dishes.description: Flatbread made from grated coconut, flour, and salt — lightly grilled and served with sambol or dhal
source_files: Srilanka/Mr. Shishir_ Sri Lanka_ All Inclusive Guide.pdf

local_dishes.name: Watalappan
local_dishes.description: A creamy steamed pudding made with coconut milk, jaggery, eggs, and cardamom — Sri Lanka's take on crème caramel
source_files: Srilanka/Mr. Shishir_ Sri Lanka_ All Inclusive Guide.pdf

local_dishes.name: Coconut Pancakes (Pol Pani Pancakes)
local_dishes.description: Sweet coconut and jaggery filling rolled in a soft crepe — perfect evening snack
source_files: Srilanka/Mr. Shishir_ Sri Lanka_ All Inclusive Guide.pdf

local_dishes.name: Rice & Veg Curry
local_dishes.description: Sri Lanka's classic meal — steamed rice served with a variety of vegetable curries like pumpkin, beetroot, beans, jackfruit, brinjal, and dhal.

local_dishes.name: Hoppers
local_dishes.description: Sri Lankan answer to the pancake made from fermented rice flour, coconut milk and sugar

local_dishes.name: Vegetable Kottu
local_dishes.description: Chopped roti stir-fried with vegetables, egg (optional), and spices.

local_dishes.name: Parippu (Dhal Curry)
local_dishes.description: Creamy red lentil curry cooked in coconut milk and tempered spices. Comforting and widely available.

local_dishes.name: Brinjal (Eggplant) Moju
local_dishes.description: Sweet, tangy, and slightly spicy caramelized eggplant pickle. A flavorful side dish.

local_dishes.name: Mallung
local_dishes.description: Finely chopped leafy greens (like gotu kola or spinach) mixed with coconut and mild spices. Light, healthy, and very traditional.

local_dishes.name: Jackfruit Curry (Polos / Kos)
local_dishes.description: Young jackfruit cooked in rich spices and coconut milk. Has a texture similar to pulled meat — very filling.

local_dishes.name: Sri Lanka Rice and Curry
local_dishes.description: The quintessential dish of the island and usually is eaten at lunchtime

local_dishes.name: Fish ambul thiyal
local_dishes.description: Sour fish curry - fish cut into cubes, sauteed in spices including black pepper, cinnamon, turmeric, garlic, pandan leaves and curry leaves with dried goraka

local_dishes.name: Kottu
local_dishes.description: Made with godamba roti (flat, crispy bread) that is fried and chopped with selected ingredients

local_dishes.name: Kukul mas curry
local_dishes.description: Chicken curry with spices like fennel seeds, cardamom, cloves and cinnamon sticks tempered in hot oil

local_dishes.name: Parippu
local_dishes.description: Dhal curry - the most common curry in all of Sri Lankan cuisine

local_dishes.name: Lamprais
local_dishes.description: Combination of meat, rice and sambol chili sauce, wrapped into a banana leaf packet and steamed

local_dishes.name: Polos
local_dishes.description: Green jackfruit curry - fruit sliced and boiled, cooked with onions, garlic, ginger and spices

local_dishes.name: Wambatu moju
local_dishes.description: Eggplant/brinjals pickle - extremely flavorful candied eggplant pickle served with rice and curries

local_dishes.name: Gotu kola sambol
local_dishes.description: Pennywort salad - medicinal herb shredded and combined with shallots, tomatoes, fresh grated coconut and chili

local_dishes.name: Pol Roti
local_dishes.description: Similar taste to naan, made with wheat flour and scraped coconut, served warm

local_dishes.name: Ceylon Tea
local_dishes.description: Tea usually served black with milk and kithul jaggery - a cube of brown refined sugar

local_dishes.name: Kola Kanda
local_dishes.description: Traditional green, herby concoction believed to help digestion, enhance immune system and reduce cholesterol levels

local_dishes.name: Toddy and Arrack
local_dishes.description: Toddy is alcoholic drink from palm tree sap, Arrack is further fermented version of Toddy

local_dishes.name: Asmi
local_dishes.description: Rice flour with milk, kneaded and made into batter with coconut milk and cinnamon leaves, deep fried and garnished with treacle syrup

safety_tips.tip: Carry a copy of your passport; keep the original in a hotel safe

safety_tips.tip: Avoid displaying expensive jewelry or electronics in public
safety_tips._source_file: Srilanka/Mr. Shishir_ Sri Lanka_ All Inclusive Guide.pdf

safety_tips.tip: Be cautious with street food — eat at clean, busy stalls
safety_tips._source_file: Srilanka/Mr. Shishir_ Sri Lanka_ All Inclusive Guide.pdf

safety_tips.tip: Be aware of your surroundings in crowded places and tourist spots
safety_tips._source_file: Srilanka/Mr. Shishir_ Sri Lanka_ All Inclusive Guide.pdf

safety_tips.tip: Keep cash and cards in separate pouches; avoid carrying large amounts
safety_tips._source_file: Srilanka/Mr. Shishir_ Sri Lanka_ All Inclusive Guide.pdf

safety_tips.tip: Follow local laws and customs — respect religious sites and dress codes
safety_tips._source_file: Srilanka/Mr. Shishir_ Sri Lanka_ All Inclusive Guide.pdf

safety_tips.tip: Use bottled or filtered water; avoid tap water
safety_tips._source_file: Srilanka/Mr. Shishir_ Sri Lanka_ All Inclusive Guide.pdf

safety_tips.tip: Take precautions against mosquitoes — wear repellent
safety_tips._source_file: Srilanka/Mr. Shishir_ Sri Lanka_ All Inclusive Guide.pdf

safety_tips.tip: Keep emergency contacts handy (local police, embassy, hotel)
safety_tips._source_file: Srilanka/Mr. Shishir_ Sri Lanka_ All Inclusive Guide.pdf

safety_tips.tip: Avoid swimming in unmonitored beaches during rough seas or rainy seasons
safety_tips._source_file: Srilanka/Mr. Shishir_ Sri Lanka_ All Inclusive Guide.pdf

safety_tips.tip: Taxi or tuk-tuk overcharging – always agree on a fare beforehand or use a meter/app
safety_tips._source_file: Srilanka/Mr. Shishir_ Sri Lanka_ All Inclusive Guide.pdf

safety_tips.tip: Fake gem shops – some tourists are lured to overpriced "official" shops
safety_tips._source_file: Srilanka/Mr. Shishir_ Sri Lanka_ All Inclusive Guide.pdf

safety_tips.tip: Street hawkers overcharging – be cautious when buying souvenirs or crafts
safety_tips._source_file: Srilanka/Mr. Shishir_ Sri Lanka_ All Inclusive Guide.pdf

safety_tips.tip: Money exchange scams – always use authorized money changers or banks
safety_tips._source_file: Srilanka/Mr. Shishir_ Sri Lanka_ All Inclusive Guide.pdf

safety_tips.tip: Timeshare / holiday club pitches – often aggressive at beaches and tourist spots
safety_tips._source_file: Srilanka/Mr. Shishir_ Sri Lanka_ All Inclusive Guide.pdf

safety_tips.tip: Fake charity or donation requests – especially near temples or popular sites
safety_tips._source_file: Srilanka/Mr. Shishir_ Sri Lanka_ All Inclusive Guide.pdf

safety_tips.tip: "Free" tours or services – may lead to forced purchases later
safety_tips._source_file: Srilanka/Mr. Shishir_ Sri Lanka_ All Inclusive Guide.pdf

safety_tips.tip: Pickpocketing – common in crowded places, markets, and buses
safety_tips._source_file: Srilanka/Mr. Shishir_ Sri Lanka_ All Inclusive Guide.pdf

safety_tips.tip: Overpriced guided tours – compare prices and check reviews before booking
safety_tips._source_file: Srilanka/Mr. Shishir_ Sri Lanka_ All Inclusive Guide.pdf

safety_tips.tip: Fake police or officials asking for fines – ask for proper ID before paying
safety_tips._source_file: Srilanka/Mr. Shishir_ Sri Lanka_ All Inclusive Guide.pdf

safety_tips.tip: Dress modestly, especially when visiting temples — cover shoulders and knees
safety_tips._source_file: Srilanka/Mr. Shishir_ Sri Lanka_ All Inclusive Guide.pdf

safety_tips.tip: Remove shoes and hats before entering religious sites
safety_tips._source_file: Srilanka/Mr. Shishir_ Sri Lanka_ All Inclusive Guide.pdf

safety_tips.tip: Greet with a smile and use "Ayubowan" (palms together) as a respectful hello
safety_tips._source_file: Srilanka/Mr. Shishir_ Sri Lanka_ All Inclusive Guide.pdf

safety_tips.tip: Ask permission before taking photos of people, especially monks
safety_tips._source_file: Srilanka/Mr. Shishir_ Sri Lanka_ All Inclusive Guide.pdf

safety_tips.tip: Use your right hand (or both hands) for giving or receiving items
safety_tips._source_file: Srilanka/Mr. Shishir_ Sri Lanka_ All Inclusive Guide.pdf

safety_tips.tip: Respect Buddhist symbols — treat statues and images of Buddha with reverence
safety_tips._source_file: Srilanka/Mr. Shishir_ Sri Lanka_ All Inclusive Guide.pdf

safety_tips.tip: Don't touch or pose with your back to Buddha statues — it's considered disrespectful
safety_tips._source_file: Srilanka/Mr. Shishir_ Sri Lanka_ All Inclusive Guide.pdf

safety_tips.tip: Don't point your feet toward people, statues, or sacred objects
safety_tips._source_file: Srilanka/Mr. Shishir_ Sri Lanka_ All Inclusive Guide.pdf

safety_tips.tip: Don't take photos of military or government buildings
safety_tips._source_file: Srilanka/Mr. Shishir_ Sri Lanka_ All Inclusive Guide.pdf

safety_tips.tip: Don't litter — Sri Lanka is known for its natural beauty, so keep it clean
safety_tips._source_file: Srilanka/Mr. Shishir_ Sri Lanka_ All Inclusive Guide.pdf

safety_tips.tip: Don't compare Sri Lanka to India or discuss sensitive political topics
safety_tips._source_file: Srilanka/Mr. Shishir_ Sri Lanka_ All Inclusive Guide.pdf

safety_tips.tip: Don't buy or carry Buddha tattoos or souvenirs showing Buddha's image below the waist (can cause trouble at customs)
safety_tips._source_file: Srilanka/Mr. Shishir_ Sri Lanka_ All Inclusive Guide.pdf

safety_tips.tip: Avoid isolated beaches or streets late at night.
safety_tips._source_file: Srilanka/Mr. Gagandeep_All Inclusive Guide_Srilanka.pdf

safety_tips.tip: Use registered taxis or hotel-arranged transport.
safety_tips._source_file: Srilanka/Mr. Gagandeep_All Inclusive Guide_Srilanka.pdf

safety_tips.tip: Keep valuables discreet and use hotel safes.
safety_tips._source_file: Srilanka/Mr. Gagandeep_All Inclusive Guide_Srilanka.pdf

safety_tips.tip: Carry small denominations of cash.
safety_tips._source_file: Srilanka/Mr. Gagandeep_All Inclusive Guide_Srilanka.pdf

safety_tips.tip: Exchange money at authorized counters or banks.
safety_tips._source_file: Srilanka/Mr. Gagandeep_All Inclusive Guide_Srilanka.pdf

safety_tips.tip: Always check change carefully.
safety_tips._source_file: Srilanka/Mr. Gagandeep_All Inclusive Guide_Srilanka.pdf

safety_tips.tip: Politely decline unsolicited help at ATMs.
safety_tips._source_file: Srilanka/Mr. Gagandeep_All Inclusive Guide_Srilanka.pdf

safety_tips.tip: Be aware of taxi / tuk-tuk overcharging.
safety_tips._source_file: Srilanka/Mr. Gagandeep_All Inclusive Guide_Srilanka.pdf

safety_tips.tip: Beware of 'Temple is Closed' scam where unofficial guides approach tourists and later demand high payment.

safety_tips.tip: Avoid gemstone scams: fake or overpriced stones sold without certification.

safety_tips.tip: Check prices for beach & water sports before service to avoid overpricing.
safety_tips._source_file: Srilanka/Mr. Gagandeep_All Inclusive Guide_Srilanka.pdf

safety_tips.tip: Check bar & club bills carefully for extra items added.
safety_tips._source_file: Srilanka/Mr. Gagandeep_All Inclusive Guide_Srilanka.pdf

connectivity_tips.tip: Dialog has largest coverage nationwide
connectivity_tips._source_file: Srilanka/Mr. Shishir_ Sri Lanka_ All Inclusive Guide.pdf

connectivity_tips.tip: Mobitel offers strong rural and hill area coverage
connectivity_tips._source_file: Srilanka/Mr. Shishir_ Sri Lanka_ All Inclusive Guide.pdf

connectivity_tips.tip: Airtel has affordable plans with good urban coverage
connectivity_tips._source_file: Srilanka/Mr. Shishir_ Sri Lanka_ All Inclusive Guide.pdf

connectivity_tips.tip: Hutch is budget-friendly but has limited remote coverage
connectivity_tips._source_file: Srilanka/Mr. Shishir_ Sri Lanka_ All Inclusive Guide.pdf

connectivity_tips.tip: SIM cards available at Colombo Airport, city stores, and official outlets
connectivity_tips._source_file: Srilanka/Mr. Shishir_ Sri Lanka_ All Inclusive Guide.pdf

connectivity_tips.tip: Requirements for SIM card: Passport (original), sometimes visa
connectivity_tips._source_file: Srilanka/Mr. Shishir_ Sri Lanka_ All Inclusive Guide.pdf

connectivity_tips.tip: Prepaid SIM cost ~ LKR 200–500; data packs extra
connectivity_tips._source_file: Srilanka/Mr. Shishir_ Sri Lanka_ All Inclusive Guide.pdf

connectivity_tips.tip: Short-term tourist packs: 1–7 days: ~1–5 GB (LKR 500–1000), 30 days: ~5–10 GB (LKR 1500–3000)
connectivity_tips._source_file: Srilanka/Mr. Shishir_ Sri Lanka_ All Inclusive Guide.pdf

connectivity_tips.tip: Top-up available at stores, kiosks, or apps
connectivity_tips._source_file: Srilanka/Mr. Shishir_ Sri Lanka_ All Inclusive Guide.pdf

connectivity_tips.tip: Wi-Fi is common, but mobile data is more reliable
connectivity_tips._source_file: Srilanka/Mr. Shishir_ Sri Lanka_ All Inclusive Guide.pdf

connectivity_tips.tip: Carry a power bank for long trips or safaris
connectivity_tips._source_file: Srilanka/Mr. Shishir_ Sri Lanka_ All Inclusive Guide.pdf

connectivity_tips.tip: eSIM-friendly providers include Dialog Axiata, Mobitel (SLT-Mobitel), Hutch & Airtel.
Buy SIM cards at Bandaranaike International Airport arrivals - official kiosks for Dialog, Mobitel, Airtel, Hutch.
Retail stores in cities like Colombo, Kandy, Galle sell tourist SIMs at telecom outlets and electronics shops.
Passport required for SIM registration (mandatory).
Dialog has largest coverage nationwide
Mobitel has strong rural and hill area coverage
Airtel offers affordable plans with good urban coverage
Hutch is budget-friendly but has limited remote coverage
SIM cards available at Colombo Airport, city stores, and official outlets
Requirements for SIM: Passport (original), sometimes visa
Prepaid SIM costs ~ LKR 200–500; data packs extra
Short-term tourist packs: 1–7 days: ~1–5 GB (LKR 500–1000)
Top-up available at stores, kiosks, or apps
Wi-Fi is common, but mobile data is more reliable
Carry a power bank for long trips or safaris

There are 3 main SIM Card providers in Sri Lanka, but Dialog is recommended for better coverage. Best place to buy a sim card is at the airport kiosks. Expect to pay 1,800 LKR for a Dialog Sim which comes with 30 GB of data with a validity period of 30 days.
Even if you do not purchase an international roaming plan for your current India number, remember to activate roaming so that you receive messages (important for things like OTPs).

health_tips.tip: Keep routine vaccines up to date (MMR, DTP, Polio)
health_tips._source_file: Srilanka/Mr. Shishir_ Sri Lanka_ All Inclusive Guide.pdf

health_tips.tip: Recommended vaccines: Hepatitis A & B, Typhoid, Rabies (if animal contact), Tetanus booster

health_tips.tip: Drink bottled/filtered water; avoid street food if unsure

health_tips.tip: Use mosquito repellent to prevent dengue, chikungunya, malaria
health_tips._source_file: Srilanka/Mr. Shishir_ Sri Lanka_ All Inclusive Guide.pdf

health_tips.tip: Protect from sun: sunscreen, hat, sunglasses

health_tips.tip: Carry basic medicines and first-aid kit
health_tips._source_file: Srilanka/Mr. Shishir_ Sri Lanka_ All Inclusive Guide.pdf

health_tips.tip: Travel insurance is strongly advised
health_tips._source_file: Srilanka/Mr. Shishir_ Sri Lanka_ All Inclusive Guide.pdf

health_tips.tip: Emergency contacts: Ambulance 1990, Red Cross +94 11 268 6090
health_tips._source_file: Srilanka/Mr. Shishir_ Sri Lanka_ All Inclusive Guide.pdf

health_tips.tip: Carry prescriptions in original packaging + digital copy
health_tips._source_file: Srilanka/Mr. Shishir_ Sri Lanka_ All Inclusive Guide.pdf

health_tips.tip: Pack basic medicines (fever, allergy, stomach upset).
Bring motion sickness tablets for hill routes like Ella.
Use mosquito repellent - important for tropical climate.
Pack sunscreen & after-sun gel for sun protection.
Carry hand sanitizer and ORS sachets.
Sri Lanka has good private healthcare facilities in cities like Colombo and Kandy.
Keep routine vaccines up to date (MMR, DTP, Polio)
Drink bottled/filtered water; avoid street food if unsure
Use mosquito repellent to prevent dengue, chikungunya, malaria
Protect from sun: sunscreen, hat, sunglasses
Carry basic medicines and first-aid kit
Travel insurance is strongly advised
Carry prescriptions in original packaging + digital copy
Travel Insurance recommended - TATA AIG. Carry doctors prescriptions/travel prescriptions for medicines (except over the counter meds).
Carry mosquito repellent as recommended item.

source_files: Srilanka/Mr. Shishir_ Sri Lanka_ All Inclusive Guide.pdf
